text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID:          SP-XXXX-XXXXX
Accession Number:     [Accession pending assignment at time of transcription]
Ordering Physician:   [Gastroenterology / Interventional Endoscopy Service]
Clinical Service:     Gastroenterology / Gastrointestinal Oncology
Submitting Physician: [EUS Proceduralist, Gastroenterology]
Date of Procedure:    [Date]
Date Received:        [Date]
Date Reported:        [Date]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Patient is a 66-year-old female with a 3-month history of progressive epigastric pain radiating to the back, significant unintentional weight loss (~14 lbs), new-onset worsening of previously well-controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus (fasting glucoses 220-280 mg/dL despite medication adjustment, HbA1c 9.2%), early satiety, and intermittent nausea. Serum CA 19-9 markedly elevated at 1,200 U/mL; CEA 5.8 ng/mL. Cross-sectional imaging (CT abdomen/pelvis, pancreatic protocol) demonstrated a 3.4 cm hypoattenuating mass in the pancreatic head/uncinate process with poorly defined margins, abutment of the superior mesenteric artery (SMA) >180 degrees, and superior mesenteric vein (SMV) narrowing (~40% luminal compromise). PET-CT confirmed hypermetabolic activity within the pancreatic head mass (SUVmax 9.2) without distant metastatic disease. Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS)-guided fine needle biopsy (FNB) performed for tissue diagnosis prior to multidisciplinary treatment planning.

Clinical working diagnosis: Pancreatic head/uncinate process mass, borderline resectable by NCCN criteria. Rule out adenocarcinoma.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
A.  EUS-FNB, pancreatic head mass, passes 1-3 (three separate needle passes performed; 22-gauge Franseen needle; material submitted in formalin for cell block and smear preparation)
B.  EUS-FNB, pancreatic head mass, rapid on-site evaluation (ROSE) smear (Diff-Quik and Papanicolaou stains)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number, and designated "EUS-FNB pancreatic head." The specimen consists of multiple tan-white to hemorrhagic tissue cores and fragments in aggregate measuring up to 0.8 cm in greatest dimension. The material is entirely submitted for histologic sections following standard formalin fixation and paraffin embedding. Cell block prepared from needle rinse material. Representative sections examined.

Specimen B: On-site evaluation smears prepared at the time of procedure. Four air-dried smears stained with Diff-Quik; four alcohol-fixed smears stained with Papanicolaou technique. Material adequate for evaluation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTRAOPERATIVE / RAPID ON-SITE EVALUATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ROSE Interpretation (Specimen B): POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS. Cellular smears demonstrating clusters of atypical epithelial cells with enlarged nuclei, irregular nuclear membranes, prominent nucleoli, and increased nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Background desmoplastic stroma noted. Preliminary interpretation communicated to the endoscopist at the time of procedure. Adequate cellularity confirmed; additional passes obtained for cell block and immunohistochemical studies.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Histologic sections from the cell block (Specimen A) reveal fragments of fibrotic stroma containing infiltrating neoplastic glandular structures. The tumor is composed of irregular, angulated, and haphazardly arranged glands and duct-like structures of varying caliber embedded within a prominent desmoplastic stroma. Individual tumor cells are columnar to cuboidal with moderately enlarged nuclei, irregular nuclear contours, coarsely granular chromatin, and conspicuous, often large nucleoli. Nuclear pleomorphism is present and assessed as moderate (Grade 2). Scattered atypical mitotic figures are identified. The cytoplasm is pale to eosinophilic with focal intracytoplasmic and intraluminal mucin production, highlighted by alcian blue/PAS staining. An infiltrative growth pattern is evident with small irregular glands dissecting through fibrotic stroma — a pattern inconsistent with normal pancreatic ductal epithelium and diagnostic of invasive adenocarcinoma.

Specifically noted architectural features include:
- Incomplete luminal formation with "naked" glands lacking surrounding lobular architecture
- Nuclear-to-stromal disproportion with the neoplastic glands present in hypocellular desmoplastic zones
- Focal perineural-type tropism within the sampled tissue cores (inconclusive given the limited biopsy material)
- Absence of a surrounding normal lobular pancreatic architecture adjacent to the neoplastic glands

No normal acinar tissue or islets of Langerhans identified within the sampled material. No high-grade pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN-3) definitively identified in the limited sample, though this is expected given the FNB technique.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Immunohistochemical stains were performed on cell block sections using standard protocols with appropriate positive and negative controls. Results are as follows:

  CK7:             POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic glands)
  CK19:            POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining)
  CK20:            NEGATIVE
  CDX2:            NEGATIVE (excludes lower GI/colorectal primary)
  MUC1:            POSITIVE (apical and cytoplasmic, diffuse)
  MUC5AC:          FOCALLY POSITIVE (luminal, subset of neoplastic cells)
  MUC2:            NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin:   NEGATIVE (excludes neuroendocrine differentiation)
  Chromogranin A:  NEGATIVE (excludes neuroendocrine differentiation)
  SOX11:           Not performed
  p53:             ABERRANT EXPRESSION — strong, diffuse nuclear overexpression pattern observed in >80% of neoplastic cell nuclei, consistent with gain-of-function TP53 mutation (as opposed to the null/complete loss pattern); loss-of-function cannot be excluded on this staining pattern alone, and molecular correlation is recommended
  SMAD4/DPC4:      RETAINED (positive nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells, equivalent to internal stromal cell staining; no loss of expression)
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):  Approximately 30% proliferative index (hot spot estimation in areas of highest tumor density)

Note: Additional IHC stains may be added as clinically indicated. Correlation with molecular studies is recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Molecular / NGS testing: Material from this specimen has been submitted for next-generation sequencing (solid tumor panel) per oncology request. Results PENDING at the time of this report. Results will be issued as an addendum upon completion.

Germline testing: Germline genetic counseling and testing referral placed per oncology team; results to be reported separately.

Mismatch repair (MMR) immunohistochemistry by IHC (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2): PENDING — stains in progress; results will be issued as an addendum. Clinical correlation with molecular MSI/MMR status is recommended for treatment planning purposes, including assessment of eligibility for immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy.

FISH / KRAS mutation-specific testing: Not performed as a standalone assay; deferred to NGS panel.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
A.  EUS-GUIDED FINE NEEDLE BIOPSY, PANCREATIC HEAD MASS:

    ADENOCARCINOMA, PANCREATIC DUCTAL TYPE
    - MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (HISTOLOGIC GRADE 2)
    - IMMUNOPHENOTYPE CONSISTENT WITH PANCREATIC DUCTAL PRIMARY
      (CK7+, CK19+, MUC1+, MUC5AC focally+; CK20-, CDX2-, synaptophysin-,
      chromogranin-; SMAD4/DPC4 retained; p53 aberrant overexpression)
    - SEE MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION AND ANCILLARY STUDIES
    - MOLECULAR/NGS RESULTS: PENDING (addendum to follow)
    - MMR IHC RESULTS: PENDING (addendum to follow)

B.  RAPID ON-SITE EVALUATION SMEARS, PANCREATIC HEAD MASS:
    POSITIVE FOR MALIGNANT CELLS, CONSISTENT WITH ADENOCARCINOMA
    (see above; smear interpretation superseded by cell block histology)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The histomorphologic and immunohistochemical findings are diagnostic of a moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma arising in the pancreatic head/uncinate region. The immunophenotype (CK7+/CK19+/MUC1+/CK20-/CDX2-) is consistent with a pancreatic ductal primary and effectively excludes a lower gastrointestinal, colorectal, or neuroendocrine primary. The retained SMAD4/DPC4 expression by IHC is a prognostically relevant finding, as loss of SMAD4/DPC4 has been associated with a higher propensity for widespread metastatic dissemination in pancreatic cancer; retained expression may correlate with more locoregionally confined disease behavior, though this correlation is imperfect. The aberrant p53 overexpression pattern by IHC is consistent with an underlying gain-of-function TP53 missense mutation (molecular confirmation pending with NGS panel).

The Ki-67 proliferative index of approximately 30% is consistent with moderate-to-high proliferative activity, as expected for a grade 2 pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

Findings have been discussed with the referring gastroenterology/oncology service. Multidisciplinary tumor board review is recommended.

NOTE: This report represents the final histopathologic diagnosis based on currently available material. Addendum(a) will be issued upon receipt of pending molecular/NGS and MMR IHC results.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNATURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Attending Pathologist: [Name, MD], Gastrointestinal and Hepatobiliary Pathology
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]

Electronically signed and verified: [Date/Time]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DISTRIBUTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Copies transmitted to: Ordering Gastroenterology Service; Gastrointestinal Medical Oncology; Multidisciplinary Pancreatic Cancer Tumor Board
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
END OF REPORT
